item.title: He Took 6-Year-Old's Live Over a Potty Accident
item.description: Martin didn't live with a monster under his bed, he lived with a monster who posed as someone who was meant to protect him... This case is both chilling and heartbreaking. What are your thoughts? - Em x

media:content.media:title: He Took 6-Year-Old's Live Over a Potty Accident
media:content.media:description: Martin didn't live with a monster under his bed, he lived with a monster who posed as someone who was meant to protect him... This case is both chilling and heartbreaking. What are your thoughts? - Em x

item.title: The Footage at the Gym Revealed What He DID to His Son!
item.description: Christopher Gregor was convicted over the death of his 6-year-old son, Corey Micciolo, in New Jersey. The case drew national attention after surveillance video showed Gregor forcing Corey to run on a treadmill at high speed, causing the boy to fall repeatedly. Days later, Corey became seriously ill and died in April 2021. Prosecutors argued he had suffered ongoing abuse, and medical experts said his death was a homicide caused by blunt-force injuries. In 2024, Gregor was found guilty of aggravated manslaughter and child endangerment, then sentenced to 25 years in prison. The case horrified many because warnings about Corey’s safety had reportedly been raised before his death.

media:content.media:title: The Footage at the Gym Revealed What He DID to His Son!
media:content.media:description: Christopher Gregor was convicted over the death of his 6-year-old son, Corey Micciolo, in New Jersey. The case drew national attention after surveillance video showed Gregor forcing Corey to run on a treadmill at high speed, causing the boy to fall repeatedly. Days later, Corey became seriously ill and died in April 2021. Prosecutors argued he had suffered ongoing abuse, and medical experts said his death was a homicide caused by blunt-force injuries. In 2024, Gregor was found guilty of aggravated manslaughter and child endangerment, then sentenced to 25 years in prison. The case horrified many because warnings about Corey’s safety had reportedly been raised before his death.

item.title: It Was a Call from God: The Horror of the Turpin Family
item.description: David and Louise Turpin were a California couple who, for years, abused and imprisoned their 13 children inside their Perris home. The siblings were starved, beaten, isolated, and often chained to beds or furniture in filthy conditions. Despite their ages, several were so malnourished they looked much younger. In January 2018, their 17-year-old daughter escaped through a window and called 911, leading police to uncover the abuse. Officers found the children living in extreme neglect, with little food, no education, and severe physical and psychological trauma. The case shocked the world because the family had maintained a carefully staged public image while hiding years of cruelty behind closed doors.

media:content.media:title: It Was a Call from God: The Horror of the Turpin Family
media:content.media:description: David and Louise Turpin were a California couple who, for years, abused and imprisoned their 13 children inside their Perris home. The siblings were starved, beaten, isolated, and often chained to beds or furniture in filthy conditions. Despite their ages, several were so malnourished they looked much younger. In January 2018, their 17-year-old daughter escaped through a window and called 911, leading police to uncover the abuse. Officers found the children living in extreme neglect, with little food, no education, and severe physical and psychological trauma. The case shocked the world because the family had maintained a carefully staged public image while hiding years of cruelty behind closed doors.
